---
title: "How the Project Worked"
description: "A reader-friendly summary of the research workflow behind the working paper."
---

This page explains the workflow behind the project in ordinary terms. It is
not the full methodology paper. It is a map of how the working paper moved from
question, to sources, to evidence, to claims, to review, to public release.

## The Basic Idea

The project treated the paper as the visible result of a larger research record.
That record includes source files, evidence rows, claim maps, reviews,
limitations, render checks, and human approval points.

The goal was not to make interpretation mechanical. The goal was to make the
path from source to claim easier to inspect.

::: {.callout-note title="Methodology working paper"}
This page is the short version. The detailed methodology paper,
[The Scholarly Build System](methodology-paper/methodology-paper.html),
explains the governed human-AI method as a public methodology working paper.
It is not journal-final, universally validated, or independent replication
evidence; it evaluates one developmental case study.
:::

## Workflow At A Glance

| Step | What Happened | Why It Matters |
|---|---|---|
| Research question | The project began with a question about how the Gettysburg Address turns soldier death into public obligation. | Keeps the inquiry focused. |
| Corpus building | Primary sources, variants, reception evidence, and secondary-source targets were registered with provenance and verification status. | Shows what evidence is being used and what remains limited. |
| Source preservation | Raw and normalized files were kept separate, with hashes and retrieval notes where available. | Reduces silent source drift. |
| Evidence coding | Passages were entered into an evidence matrix with observations, interpretations, alternative readings, confidence, and review status. | Separates source text from interpretation. |
| Claim mapping | Main claims were linked to supporting and disconfirming evidence. | Makes the argument traceable. |
| Drafting | The working paper was drafted from the approved claims and evidence. | Keeps prose tied to the research record. |
| Review | Historical, rhetorical, theological, source, countervoice, anachronism, coding, and publication reviews tested the draft. | Catches overstatement and weak links before publication. |
| Publication package | The paper, audit, reproducibility report, evidence appendix, and limitations were assembled. | Defines what kind of release this is. |
| Human approval | The scholar approved public working-paper release with disclosed limitations. | Keeps publication authority human. |

## What The Evidence Table Does

The evidence table is not a proof machine. It is a browsing and audit tool.
Each row shows a source passage and separates:

- what the source says;
- what the project observes in that passage;
- what interpretation is drawn from it;
- what alternative reading remains possible;
- whether the row is disconfirming;
- how confident the project is in using it.

That structure helps readers see where the paper is strongly grounded and
where caution remains appropriate.

## What The Reviews Do

The review pages show how the paper was challenged before release. They ask
questions such as:

- Does the paper overgeneralize from Gettysburg to war as such?
- Does it treat public rhetoric as private belief?
- Does it import later concepts into an 1863 speech?
- Are quotations and source caveats visible?
- Do counterarguments narrow the claims?

The reviews matter because the final paper is not just a polished thesis. It is
a thesis that was tested, narrowed, and released with limits still visible.

## What AI Did And Did Not Do

AI assisted with bounded research and engineering tasks: organizing files,
drafting structured artifacts, creating review passes, running checks, and
helping maintain the site.

AI did not approve the corpus, settle the thesis, authorize publication, or
replace the scholar's judgment. Those decisions remained human control points.

## What The Checks Prove

The repository checks prove limited but useful things:

- required files and schemas are present;
- references between claims and evidence are structurally valid;
- tests pass;
- Quarto can render the public site and papers;
- the public package can be reproduced from the repository state.

They do not prove that the interpretation is true. They prove that the public
argument has an inspectable evidentiary trail.

## What Still Remains Limited

The public paper remains a working paper. The main limits are:

- the Bliss Copy transcription should receive a second manuscript-image check
  before quotation-final use;
- Everett pamphlet quotations should be checked against page images;
- the Harrisburg *Patriot and Union* criticism remains a derivative
  transcription pending original newspaper image review;
- secondary-source engagement should be broadened before journal submission.

These limits are part of the public record, not hidden cleanup notes.

## Where To Go Next

- [Read the working paper](working-paper.html)
- [Browse the evidence table](evidence.html)
- [Read the audit and review results](reviews/)
- [Review the publication audit](publication-audit.html)
- [Read the full methodology paper](methodology-paper/methodology-paper.html)
